assertions.title: Green indicator for valid tokens

steps.expected: Settings page with OAuth Connections section
steps.observed: Settings page loaded with OAuth Connections section showing copilot (Not connected) and codex (Not connected)

steps.action: Check CSS class definitions for oauth-status-dot-valid
steps.expected: Green background color defined for valid state
steps.observed: CSS class .oauth-status-dot-valid is defined with background: var(--green) = #34d399 and box-shadow with green glow. Verified by creating synthetic element: computed background rgb(52, 211, 153) matching #34d399.

steps.action: Check if any backend has a valid token (green dot)
steps.expected: At least one backend showing green indicator
steps.observed: Both copilot and codex backends show TokenState='missing' with class 'oauth-status-dot-missing'. No backend currently has a valid token to verify green indicator runtime behavior.

evidence.cssVerification: oauth-status-dot-valid has background: var(--green) (#34d399) = rgb(52, 211, 153) confirmed via synthetic element test. box-shadow: rgba(52, 211, 153, 0.5) 0px 0px 6px. Width/height: 10px. Border-radius: 50%.

assertions.issues: No backend currently has a valid token to verify the green indicator at runtime. CSS definitions are correct and would render green when TokenState='valid'.

assertions.title: Yellow indicator for tokens expiring soon

steps.expected: Settings page with OAuth Connections section

steps.action: Check CSS class definitions for oauth-status-dot-expiring
steps.expected: Yellow/amber background color defined for expiring state
steps.observed: CSS class .oauth-status-dot-expiring is defined with background: var(--amber) = #fbbf24 and box-shadow with amber glow. Verified by creating synthetic element: computed background rgb(251, 191, 36) matching #fbbf24.

steps.action: Check if any backend has an expiring token (yellow dot)
steps.expected: At least one backend showing yellow indicator
steps.observed: Both backends show TokenState='missing'. Cannot artificially create expiring-soon token state for visual verification.

evidence.cssVerification: oauth-status-dot-expiring has background: var(--amber) (#fbbf24) = rgb(251, 191, 36) confirmed via synthetic element test. box-shadow: rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.5) 0px 0px 6px.

assertions.issues: Cannot artificially create expiring-soon token state for visual verification. CSS definitions are correct and would render yellow/amber when TokenState='expiring'.

assertions.title: Red indicator for expired or missing tokens

steps.expected: Settings page with OAuth Connections section
steps.observed: Settings page loaded with copilot and codex backends showing 'Not connected'

steps.action: Check CSS class definitions for oauth-status-dot-expired
steps.expected: Red background color defined for expired state
steps.observed: CSS class .oauth-status-dot-expired is defined with background: var(--red) = #f87171 and box-shadow with red glow. Verified by creating synthetic element: computed background rgb(248, 113, 113) matching #f87171.

steps.action: Check CSS class definitions for oauth-status-dot-missing
steps.expected: Some visual indicator for missing token state
steps.observed: CSS class .oauth-status-dot-missing has NO explicit CSS definition. The dot renders as a 10px circle with background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0) (transparent/invisible). The 'Not connected' text is shown via .oauth-text-missing class, but the status dot itself is invisible for missing state.

steps.action: Verify what the UI shows for unauthenticated backends
steps.expected: Red or visible indicator for missing tokens
steps.observed: Both copilot and codex backends have TokenState='missing' with class 'oauth-status-dot-missing'. The computed background is rgba(0, 0, 0, 0) — the dot is invisible. Only the text 'Not connected' provides status information. No red indicator is shown for missing tokens.

cssVerification.expired: oauth-status-dot-expired: background: var(--red) (#f87171) = rgb(248, 113, 113). Correctly defined.
cssVerification.missing: oauth-status-dot-missing: NO CSS definition exists. Computed background is rgba(0, 0, 0, 0) — dot is invisible. The template applies class 'oauth-status-dot-missing' but no corresponding CSS rule sets a background color for this class.

assertions.issues: For the 'expired' state: CSS is correctly defined with red color, but no backend currently has an expired token to verify runtime behavior. For the 'missing' state: the status dot is invisible (no CSS rule for .oauth-status-dot-missing). The text 'Not connected' is shown, but there is no visual dot indicator. This is a partial gap — the 'expired' CSS is correct but the 'missing' state has no visual dot.

assertions.title: Dashboard reflects token status

steps.expected: Dashboard page showing token status for each OAuth backend
steps.observed: Dashboard loads at /ui/ with sections: Quota (openrouter - Error 401), Dashboard stats (Requests, Tokens, Avg Latency, Errors), Backends ('No backend activity yet'), Recent Requests ('No requests recorded yet'). No OAuth/token status section exists on the dashboard.

steps.action: Search dashboard content for OAuth, token, copilot, codex references
steps.observed: Dashboard text contains no references to OAuth, tokens, copilot, codex, or connected status. The 'Backends' section only tracks request activity stats, not authentication/token status.

assertions.issues: The main dashboard (/ui/) does not display OAuth token status for any backend. It only shows request statistics and quota information. Token status is only available on the /ui/settings page under 'OAuth Connections'. The assertion states 'the web dashboard at /ui/' should display token status, but this information is not present on the main dashboard.

frictions.description: The .oauth-status-dot-missing CSS class has no background color definition, making the status dot invisible for unauthenticated backends. Only text ('Not connected') provides the status.
frictions.resolution: No resolution possible — this is a CSS gap in the implementation. The dot exists in the DOM but renders transparent.

blockers.description: No backend currently has a valid (non-expired) OAuth token, preventing visual verification of the green indicator at runtime. CSS definitions exist and are correct.
Cannot artificially create an expiring-soon token state to verify the yellow/amber indicator at runtime. CSS definitions exist and are correct.
No backend has an expired token (vs missing). The expired state CSS is correctly defined, but cannot be verified at runtime. The missing state has no visual dot indicator (invisible).

The main dashboard (/ui/) does not display OAuth token status for backends. Token status information is only available on the /ui/settings page. The assertion specifically references /ui/ (dashboard), not /ui/settings.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 0 passed, 0 failed, 4 blocked. All visual indicator CSS classes are correctly defined (green=#34d399, amber=#fbbf24, red=#f87171) but no live OAuth backend has a valid/expired token to verify runtime behavior. The missing state (.oauth-status-dot-missing) has no background color, rendering the dot invisible. The main dashboard (/ui/) does not display token status — this info only exists on /ui/settings.
